description_long: This house features a complex, multi-gabled roof with dark shingles and a light-colored exterior, giving it a stately and substantial appearance. The front facade is defined by two prominent projecting sections flanking a central entryway, which is sheltered by a large overhanging gable supported by columns. The structure appears to be a single-story residence with a sprawling footprint, including several wings and extensions that create an L-shaped layout. Windows are arranged asymmetrically, with some sections featuring large rectangular openings and others with smaller, more traditional windows. The roofline is varied, with intersecting gables and hips adding architectural interest and depth. The overall design suggests a blend of modern suburban and traditional American residential styles, with a focus on spaciousness and curb appeal.
description: A large, single-story house with a complex, multi-gabled dark roof and a light exterior, featuring prominent front projections and an expansive, L-shaped layout. The entryway is sheltered by a grand gable and columns, with asymmetrical window placement and a blend of modern and traditional design elements.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the central entryway with a large gable and columns, flanked by two projecting sections, giving the house a symmetrical and inviting appearance.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the intersecting gables and hips of the roof, as well as a large rectangular window and the depth of the house's extensions.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the L-shaped layout, with visible roof slopes and a partially open section exposing interior framing and a window.
description_view_3: The opposite rear/side view displays more of the roof's complexity, with intersecting gables and visible open wall sections, emphasizing the house's multi-winged structure.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entryway with a large gable overhang, supported by columns, and a recessed porch area.
